authorized: 2026-07-11 by Jordan ('go') after the G-LO synthesis (c9338de)
host: RunPod SECURE RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Workstation Edition (sm_120, 96GB), pod hpj0pdvk69m8m1, driver 610

design.subject: Qwen/Qwen3-30B-A3B, seq 256, sample_packing, seed 42, prediction_loss_only (committed deviation), 150 steps, eval_steps 50
design.floor_def: spread of the 3 whole-arm final eval_loss = max - min (report 2*stdev too)
design.primary_criterion: PASS iff |routed_final - mean(3 whole finals)| <= floor_spread
design.warmup_rationale: the OLMoE-hi cold-first-arm contaminated its floor (0.022, one-sided); a throwaway warm-up before arm 1 keeps all 3 whole arms warm-equivalent

step_count_change: 150 steps, NOT the original lo-config's 50. Reason: comparability to BOTH 150-step references (the Qwen3-hi primary FAIL and the OLMoE dose-response). The criterion is floor-relative and measured fresh from THIS leg's whole arms, so step count does not bias PASS/FAIL. Recorded as a deliberate, pre-data change.

rf_prediction.note: Phase-0 Qwen3-30B E=128 measured 0.687 at eff256; the hi leg's packing ran rf optimistic (0.80->0.97) so expect the same direction — measured rf may come in >0.70

discriminator.H_DISCRETE: routed gap ~= 0.06 (same as the hi FAIL) regardless of the higher fill -> per-step corruption, horizon-driven
discriminator.H_FILL: routed gap >> 0.06, scaling with the ~30% fill mass -> fill-mass-driven
discriminator.horizon_readout_free: the routed arm's 50/100/150 intermediate evals give within-run horizon dependence at no extra arm cost (evals dominate wall, so a separate short-horizon arm is not cost-justified)

fail_interpretation: per prereg_qwen3_lo_fail_interp.json: FAIL = fill pathway scales with dose -> routed stays opt-in, fix = mask the checkpointed-backward recompute dequant to routed rows. NOT abandonment.
memlock_gate_correction: build_env.sh carried a memlock>=16GB gate built on the (now REFUTED) theory that 8MB rlimit breaks pinning. The pin test proved cudaHostAlloc is not memlock-gated (16GB pins fine under 8MB). The PRO 6000 also has 8MB memlock and ran the primary 30B leg fine. Gate WAIVED for this pod; it is a false gate and should be removed from the bundle.
controls: step-0 eval fingerprint identical across all 5 arms; bleed (whole arms emit no routed INFO line, no STAGEDCNT); STAGEDCNT in routed only
cost_budget: ~$6-8 at $1.89/hr (~3h, seq256 evals dominate). ABORT any arm exceeding 90 min wall.
